per_case.user_input:
Triage shipment FRT-1042. We have a customs hold — what's wrong, what
should I do next, and draft a customer email if appropriate.
retrieved_contexts:
## Customer communication template

```
Subject: [Shipment ID] — Customs Hold Update

Dear [Consignee],

Your shipment [Shipment ID] arrived at [Port] on [Date] and was placed on
customs hold pending receipt of the Commercial Invoice from the shipper.
This is a documentation requirement, not a compliance issue.

We have contacted the shipper to request the document. We will provide
an update [within X hours / by end of day]. Should the hold extend
beyond [demurrage_starts_at], terminal demurrage charges of approximately
$[rate]/day will begin to accrue.

If you can assist in expediting the document from your supplier, please
let us know.

Best regards,
[Operations Team]
```

# SOP-001 — Customs Hold: Missing Commercial Invoice

**Owner:** Operations · Customs Desk
**Last reviewed:** 2026-02-15
**Applies to:** US, EU, UK, AU import shipments where customs has flagged a hold pending receipt of the Commercial Invoice (CI).

---

## When this SOP applies

- A customs broker or destination agent has placed the shipment on hold.
- The reason given is "missing Commercial Invoice" or "incomplete commercial documentation".
- The shipment has cleared physical inspection (or inspection is not required).

If the hold reason is anything else (HS code dispute, valuation challenge, prohibited goods), use **SOP-006 — Customs Disputes** instead. Do not apply this SOP.

## Customer holding-comm template (draft, do not auto-send)

```
Subject: [Shipment ID] — Status Update Request

Dear [Consignee],

We are tracking shipment [Shipment ID] (vessel [name], voyage [number])
and noticed that the published tracking events are out of date relative
to your expected arrival on [eta].

We have contacted [Carrier]'s operations desk to request a current
status update and revised ETA. We will provide an update within
[X hours, default 4].

If you have specific delivery commitments dependent on this shipment,
please let us know and we will prioritize.

Best regards,
[Operations Team]
```

## Email subject line format

```
[Shipment ID] — [One-line status]

Examples:
  FRT-1042 — Customs Hold Update & Expected Resolution Timeline
  FRT-1043 — Service Update: Tropical Storm Hermes
  FRT-1044 — Vessel Roll Notice
  FRT-1045 — Status Update Request
  FRT-1046 — Documentation Discrepancy Update
```

The customer's inbox should let them locate the comm by shipment ID at a glance.

## Tone calibration — the "forwarded to COO" test

Before sending any Gold or Platinum tier comm, ask:

> *If this email gets forwarded to the customer's COO without context, would I want my name on it?*

If the answer is no: revise tone, hedge more, cite more.
per_case.response:
---

## DIAGNOSIS

**Root cause:** US Customs hold issued 2026-04-26 11:05 UTC due to missing Commercial Invoice. The shipper (Aurora Textiles Pvt Ltd) has not yet provided the document; broker queue is paused. Demurrage clock is running — charges begin 2026-04-30 at $175/day. **Critical:** Brookline Apparel Co was promoted to **Platinum tier** (per correction 2026-04-27), not Gold — this escalates the SLA and communication cadence.

---

## KEY FACTS

- **Shipment:** FRT-1042 | MBL MAEU240118245 | HBL ZNVAUSNY1042 | Maersk SENTOSA V.412W
- **Status:** HELD — Customs (since 2026-04-26 11:05 UTC)
- **Missing doc:** Commercial Invoice (packing list, B/L, CoO, ISF all received)
- **Shipper:** Aurora Textiles Pvt Ltd (India) — historically slow on CI (4–6h typical response per prior note 2026-04-13)
- **Consignee:** Brookline Apparel Co (USA) — **Platinum tier** (per correction 2026-04-27), prefers 4-hour update cadence
- **Demurrage exposure:** Starts 2026-04-30 at $175/day
- **Customer SLA breach:** 2026-04-29 00:00 UTC (per shipment record)
- **Prior action:** Shipper contact attempted 2026-04-26 12:30 UTC — auto-reply received (per shipment history 2026-04-26)

---

## APPLICABLE SOPs

- **sop-customs-hold-missing-ci.md** — Diagnosis, escalation, and customer comm template for missing CI holds
- **sop-customer-comm-style-guide.md** — Required elements for incident comms to Platinum tier

---

## RECOMMENDED ACTIONS (ranked)

1. **Confirm with broker that CI is the only missing doc** — expected resolution: 15 min
   - Rationale: Per sop-customs-hold-missing-ci.md §Diagnosis checklist, brokers sometimes batch multiple issues. Verify the hold is not a HS code or valuation dispute.
   - **Action:** Email broker@continental-cb.com asking for confirmation and any additional blockers.

2. **Re-contact shipper (Aurora Textiles) with urgency** — expected resolution: 2–6 hours
   - Rationale: Prior contact (2026-04-26 12:30 UTC) got auto-reply. Shipper historically takes 4–6h to respond (per note 2026-04-13). Demurrage starts in ~3.5 days; escalate to shipper's ops manager, not just procurement.
   - **Action:** Call Aurora Textiles directly (do not rely on email alone). Emphasize demurrage clock and Platinum customer impact. Request CI within 2 hours.

3. **Notify Brookline Apparel Co (Platinum tier) immediately** — expected resolution: send within 30 min
   - Rationale: Per sop-customer-comm-style-guide.md, Platinum tier requires named exec sign-off and 4-hour update cadence. Customer SLA breach is 2026-04-29; we are already 1+ day into the hold. Use preferred contact procurement@brookline-apparel.com (per correction 2026-04-20).
   - **Action:** Send draft email below. Include demurrage exposure and next-update commitment (within 4 hours).

4. **If CI not received by 2026-04-27 18:00 UTC, escalate to shipper's management** — expected resolution: 4–8 hours
   - Rationale: Demurrage accrual is imminent. Escalate beyond procurement to Aurora Textiles' ops/finance leadership.
   - **Action:** Prepare escalation email with demurrage cost impact ($175/day × N days).

---

## DOWNSTREAM IMPACT

- **Demurrage:** Accrues starting 2026-04-30 at $175/day. If hold extends 5+ days, cost exceeds $875.
- **Customer SLA:** Platinum tier breach at 2026-04-29 00:00 UTC (per shipment record). We are already 1+ day overdue on acknowledgment.
- **Vessel cutoff:** Not applicable (cargo already discharged).

---

## DRAFT — CUSTOMER COMMUNICATION

**To:** procurement@brookline-apparel.com  
**CC:** [Operations Lead / Platinum account manager]  
**Subject:** FRT-1042 (MAEU240118245) — Customs Hold Update — Urgent

---

Dear Brookline Apparel Co,

Your shipment **FRT-1042** (MBL **MAEU240118245**, HBL **ZNVAUSNY1042**) arrived at **New York (USNYC)** on **2026-04-25** and was placed on **customs hold** on **2026-04-26 11:05 UTC** pending receipt of the **Commercial Invoice** from the shipper.

**Current Status:** The US Customs broker has flagged the shipment as held. This is a documentation requirement, not a compliance or inspection issue. All other documents (packing list, bill of lading, certificate of origin) have been received.

**What we are doing:** We have contacted the shipper (Aurora Textiles Pvt Ltd) to request the Commercial Invoice immediately. We are following up directly with their operations team to expedite the document.

**Demurrage exposure:** Terminal demurrage charges will begin to accrue on **2026-04-30** at approximately **$175 USD per day**. We are prioritizing resolution to minimize this cost.

**Next update:** We will provide a status update **within 4 hours**. If the document is received sooner, we will notify you immediately.

If you have a copy of the Commercial Invoice or can assist in expediting it from your supplier, please reply to this email or contact us directly.

Best regards,

**[Your Name]**  
Freight Operations Team  
[Company Name]  
[Phone] | [Email]

---

**Note to ops associate:** This is a **Platinum tier** customer (per correction 2026-04-27). Ensure a named account manager or ops lead is CC'd on the email. Follow up with shipper by phone within 1 hour; do not rely on email alone.

per_case.user_input:
Triage shipment FRT-1043. The Houston port is closed due to a storm.
What's our SOP for handling this and what should I do?
retrieved_contexts:
# SOP-002 — Weather-Induced Port Closure or ETA Slippage

**Owner:** Operations · Disruption Desk
**Last reviewed:** 2026-03-01
**Applies to:** Any shipment where the cause of delay is an external port event (storm, hurricane, port strike, terminal congestion declared by port authority) — not carrier-specific.

---

## When this SOP applies

- A shipment's ETA has slipped or its discharge port is closed.
- The carrier has identified the cause as external (weather, port authority, terminal fire).
- The cause is verifiable via at least one authoritative external source (NOAA, port authority bulletin, NHC, official news).

If the carrier blames "external" but no public-record confirmation exists, treat as **carrier-side** and use **SOP-007 — Carrier Schedule Disputes**.

### 3. Cite sources for every external-event claim

If you reference weather, port closure, strike, or any non-shipment-specific event: **cite the source**.

- ✅ "Per NOAA Storm Bulletin #14, Houston port is closed pending wind subsidence."
- ✅ "Per Unite the Union bulletin, work-to-rule action at Felixstowe was resolved on 26 April."
- ❌ "There's a storm in Houston so the port is closed."

This builds credibility and lets the customer verify independently.

## Diagnosis checklist

1. **Verify the event** with at least one independent authoritative source. Look up the destination port using the `external_events` tool.
2. **Get a carrier-issued revised ETA**, even a rough one. Do not invent one.
3. **Classify the event severity:**
   - **Class A (>72h closure or active hurricane warning):** Likely SLA breach for all tiers. Begin alternate-routing analysis.
   - **Class B (24–72h closure):** SLA breach for Platinum/Gold; manageable for Silver/Bronze.
   - **Class C (<24h closure):** No proactive action needed; monitor.

## Customer communication template

```
Subject: [Shipment ID] — Service Update: [Event Name]

Dear [Consignee],

We are writing to inform you of a delay affecting shipment [Shipment ID].

[Origin Port] departure was on schedule. However, [Destination Port] is
currently closed due to [event type, e.g., "Tropical Storm Hermes"].
[Source: e.g., "NOAA Storm Bulletin #14"]

Current status: [vessel anchored offshore / awaiting berth slot / etc.]
Carrier-revised ETA: [date] [or "pending; expected within 24 hours"]

This is an external event outside our and the carrier's control. We are
monitoring the situation and will update you as soon as a firm berth
window is confirmed.

If you have time-sensitive cargo, please let us know — we can evaluate
alternate routing options.
per_case.response:
---

## DIAGNOSIS

Tropical Storm Hermes has closed Houston port (USHOU) as of 2026-04-23. The vessel *ONE COMMITMENT* is safely anchored offshore awaiting berth slot. Port authority estimates reopening 27–28 Apr pending wind subsidence (NOAA Storm Bulletin #14). This is a **Class B event** (24–72h closure): ETA has slipped ~5 days from original 2026-04-23 to estimated 2026-04-28+. Demurrage exposure begins 2026-05-01 (6 days from now).

---

## KEY FACTS

- **Shipment:** FRT-1043 | 2×40GP | CNNGB → USHOU | ONE COMMITMENT V.087E
- **Customer:** Lonestar Manufacturing LLC (Silver tier, 8h SLA ack window)
- **Current status:** Vessel anchored offshore; port closed for safety (source: carrier note 2026-04-25 18:00 UTC)
- **Port reopening window:** 27–28 Apr pending wind subsidence (source: NOAA Storm Bulletin #14)
- **Demurrage exposure:** Starts 2026-05-01 at $220/day (source: downstream_constraints)
- **Customer history:** Tolerates Houston weather disruptions well; prefers single substantive comm with NOAA citation over multi-update cadence (source: recall_customer_history, ts 2026-04-18)

---

## APPLICABLE SOPs

- **sop-weather-port-closure.md** — SOP-002 governs external port closures. Classifies this as Class B (24–72h); Silver tier does not require proactive holding comm within 12h, but Lonestar's prior preference for single substantive update applies.

---

## RECOMMENDED ACTIONS (ranked)

1. **Monitor carrier for revised ETA** — expected resolution: 24–48h (by 2026-04-27 when port reopening window clarifies)  
   Rationale: Per sop-weather-port-closure.md §Recommended actions, Step 1, hold customer comm for the first 12 hours pending a carrier-issued revised ETA. Do not invent one. Carrier (ONE) has already signaled they will "rebroadcast ETA once berth slot is confirmed" (2026-04-23 05:10 UTC).

2. **Send single substantive customer comm once revised ETA is firm** — expected resolution: 1–2h after carrier ETA arrives  
   Rationale: Per sop-weather-port-closure.md §Customer communication template and Lonestar's prior preference (recall_customer_history), send one substantive update citing NOAA source rather than multiple holding comms. Include demurrage timeline if revised ETA pushes past 2026-05-01.

3. **Flag demurrage risk** — expected resolution: ongoing monitoring  
   Rationale: Demurrage clock starts 2026-05-01. If port reopens 27–28 Apr and berth slot is assigned within 24h, demurrage is avoidable. If reopening slips further, escalate to customer and explore alternate discharge ports (e.g., Mobile, Corpus Christi) per SOP-002 Class B guidance.

4. **Prepare alternate routing analysis** — expected resolution: draft by 2026-04-27 if port reopening slips  
   Rationale: If NOAA updates forecast to >72h closure, escalate to Class A and begin alternate-port evaluation. Not yet needed, but have it ready.

---

## DOWNSTREAM IMPACT

- **Demurrage exposure:** $220/day starting 2026-05-01 (6 days from now). If port reopens 27–28 Apr and berth slot is assigned within 24h, demurrage is avoidable.
- **SLA:** Silver tier (8h ack) — no proactive holding comm required within 12h per SOP-002, but Lonestar's preference for single substantive update overrides the generic rule.
- **Vessel cutoff:** None recorded; no downstream vessel constraint.

---

## NEXT STEP

**Wait for carrier to issue revised ETA** (expected by 2026-04-27 when port reopening window clarifies). Once you have it, draft the customer comm using the sop-weather-port-closure.md template, cite NOAA Bulletin #14, and include demurrage timeline if ETA pushes past 2026-05-01. Lonestar prefers one substantive update, so batch all info into a single email.

per_case.user_input:
Triage shipment FRT-1044. MSC rolled the booking. Should we re-book
with a different carrier, and what does our SOP say about this?
retrieved_contexts:
# SOP-003 — Capacity Rollover (Vessel Roll)

**Owner:** Operations · Booking Desk
**Last reviewed:** 2026-03-10
**Applies to:** Any shipment where the carrier has rolled the booking from the originally confirmed vessel to a later sailing due to capacity constraints (overbooking).

---

## When this SOP applies

- Carrier has issued a roll notice on a confirmed booking.
- The cause is carrier-side capacity, not shipper readiness or documentation.
- The roll exceeds 24 hours.

If the cause is shipper-side (cargo not ready, late documentation), this is **not** a roll — it's a *no-show* and falls under **SOP-008 — No-Show Recovery**.

### Step 2 — Decide: accept the roll, or re-book

**Re-book if any of these are true:**
- Customer is Platinum tier AND roll exceeds 48 hours.
- Customer is Gold tier AND roll causes SLA breach.
- An alternate carrier exists with on-time % >= 88 AND their ETD is before the rolled booking's new ETD AND the rate premium is <25%.

**Accept the roll if:**
- All three of the above conditions fail.
- Bill of Lading is already issued and re-papering would add >24h delay.
- Cargo is at-port and re-gating is not feasible before the alternate carrier's cutoff.

## Hard escalation triggers (always escalate)

If **any** of these are true, escalate immediately. Do not attempt to resolve.

1. **Cargo is at risk of customs seizure or forfeiture.**
2. **A High-severity HBL/MBL discrepancy** has been identified (different legal entities, cargo description mismatch). See **SOP-005**.
3. **Demurrage exposure exceeds $5,000** on any single shipment.
4. **A Platinum customer has explicitly asked to speak to "someone senior".**
5. **Carrier is refusing to honor a confirmed booking** (rare, but happens).
6. **Hazardous cargo (IMDG class 1, 6, or 7) is involved in the exception.**
7. **Force majeure has been declared** by the carrier.
8. **Legal letter, claim, or threat thereof** has been received.

Impact assessment: [The new ETA falls X days [before/after] your delivery
promise of Y / The new ETA still meets your delivery promise].

We are evaluating alternate carrier options on this lane that may restore
your original timeline. We will respond within [SLA window] with either:
  - A confirmation that the rolled booking is the best path, with a
    service-recovery proposal; or
  - An alternate-carrier proposal for your approval.

Best regards,
[Operations Team]
```

**Hedge rules:**
- Never describe an alternate as "booked" until the customer has approved AND the carrier has confirmed.
- Quote rate premiums explicitly when proposing an alternate.
- Do not share the reason ("overbooking") in a tone that blames the carrier — keep it factual.
per_case.response:
## DIAGNOSIS

MSC rolled the booking from ALPHA V.123N (ETD 2026-04-22, ETA 2026-05-25) to BETA V.124N (ETD 2026-04-29, ETA 2026-06-02) due to vessel overbooking — a **7-day delay**. This breaches the Gold-tier customer's delivery promise of 2026-05-30. Per SOP guidance, a Gold-tier customer with an SLA breach caused by a roll **should trigger re-booking evaluation**. Two viable alternates exist: Hapag-Lloyd (92% on-time, ETA 2026-05-29, +$300 premium) and CMA CGM (88% on-time, ETA 2026-06-01, +$130 premium). Hapag-Lloyd meets the re-booking criteria; CMA CGM does not (ETA after rolled booking).

---

## KEY FACTS

- **Roll details:** MSC ALPHA V.123N → MSC BETA V.124N; 7-day delay (source: carrier note 2026-04-21 22:20 UTC)
- **SLA impact:** Rolled ETA 2026-06-02 vs. customer promise 2026-05-30 = **3-day breach** (source: `downstream_constraints.customer_sla_breach_at`)
- **Customer tier:** Gold (4-hour ack SLA) (source: shipment record)
- **Customer profile:** Coffee cargo, time-sensitive; rate premium ≤$300/container acceptable for SLA protection (source: recall_customer_history 2026-04-06)
- **Vessel cutoff:** 2026-04-28 18:00 UTC — **6 days remaining** (source: `downstream_constraints.vessel_cutoff_at`)
- **Container status:** Gated in at VNSGN 2026-04-20 (source: tracking event); re-gating feasible
- **B/L status:** PENDING (source: documents field) — re-papering not a blocker
- **Hapag-Lloyd alternate:** 92% on-time (≥88 ✓), ETA 2026-05-29 (before rolled ETA ✓), rate $2,150 vs. MSC $1,850 = **+$300 premium** (<25% ✓) — **meets all re-booking criteria**
- **CMA CGM alternate:** 88% on-time (≥88 ✓), ETA 2026-06-01 (after rolled ETA ✗) — does not meet criteria
- **Lane history:** MSC has 79% on-time on VNSGN-DEHAM with "chronic vessel rollovers" (source: carrier_history)

---

## APPLICABLE SOPs

- **sop-capacity-rollover.md** — Step 2 (Re-book decision matrix), Step 3 (Customer pre-approval), Step 4 (Service recovery)

---

## RECOMMENDED ACTIONS (ranked)

1. **Propose Hapag-Lloyd re-booking to customer for written approval** — expected resolution: 2–4 hours (customer response window)  
   **Rationale:** Per sop-capacity-rollover.md §Step 2, re-book if (a) Gold tier + SLA breach ✓, (b) alternate on-time ≥88% ✓, (c) ETD before rolled booking ✓, (d) rate premium <25% ✓. All four conditions met. Hapag-Lloyd's 92% on-time and 2026-05-29 ETA restore the original promise and protect against a second roll (MSC's lane history shows chronic rollovers). The $300 premium aligns with customer's stated tolerance for SLA protection.

2. **Draft customer communication with Hapag-Lloyd proposal** — expected resolution: immediate (draft ready for ops associate to send)  
   **Rationale:** Per sop-capacity-rollover.md §Step 3, never re-book without explicit written approval when rate change >$200. Include rate premium, new ETA, and hedge language ("subject to carrier confirmation"). Acknowledge the roll and frame Hapag-Lloyd as the recommended path to restore the original timeline.

3. **If customer approves, execute Hapag-Lloyd booking and void MSC MBL** — expected resolution: 4–6 hours (carrier confirmation + B/L re-issuance)  
   **Rationale:** Per sop-capacity-rollover.md §Common pitfalls, coordinate B/L re-issuance when re-booking. Original MBL MSCUSGNHAM4412 must be voided; new Hapag-Lloyd MBL issued before container re-gates.

4. **If customer declines re-booking, prepare service-recovery proposal** — expected resolution: 2–4 hours (draft + customer negotiation)  
   **Rationale:** Per sop-capacity-rollover.md §Step 4, Gold-tier customers warrant a service-recovery gesture (rate reduction, priority handling, free doc fee) to prevent escalation. Offer a partial rate credit or priority on next booking.

---

## DOWNSTREAM IMPACT

- **SLA breach:** Rolled ETA 2026-06-02 is 3 days after customer promise 2026-05-30 (source: `customer_sla_breach_at`). Hapag-Lloyd re-booking (ETA 2026-05-29) restores compliance.
- **Vessel cutoff:** 2026-04-28 18:00 UTC — Hapag-Lloyd ETD 2026-04-26 is **2 days before cutoff** (feasible).
- **No demurrage exposure** (source: `demurrage_starts_at` is null).

---

## DRAFT — CUSTOMER COMMUNICATION

**Subject:** FRT-1044 — MSC Booking Rolled; Hapag-Lloyd Re-booking Proposal for Your Approval

Dear Hanseatic Coffee GmbH,

We regret to inform you that MSC has rolled your booking FRT-1044 (VNSGN → DEHAM, 20GP container) from vessel ALPHA V.123N to BETA V.124N due to vessel overbooking. The new ETA on MSC BETA is **2 June 2026**, which falls **3 days after** your delivery promise of 30 May.

**We recommend re-booking on Hapag-Lloyd** to restore your original timeline:

| Metric | MSC BETA (Rolled) | Hapag-Lloyd (Proposed) |
|--------|------------------|------------------------|
| **ETA Hamburg** | 2 June 2026 | **29 May 2026** |
| **Rate (20GP)** | $1,850 | $2,150 (+$300) |
| **On-time reliability** | 79% | **92%** |

The Hapag-Lloyd sailing (HL EXPRESS V.231N, ETD 26 April) departs before the MSC cutoff and restores your original delivery window. The rate premium of $300/container reflects the premium carrier's superior reliability on this lane.

**Next steps:**  
Please confirm your approval of the Hapag-Lloyd re-booking by [**time + 4 hours**] so we can secure the space and re-issue your bill of lading. If you have questions or prefer to accept the MSC roll with a service-recovery gesture, please let us know immediately.

Best regards,  
[Operations Team]

---

**Note to ops associate:** This is a **decision-support draft only**. Do not send until you have confirmed Hapag-Lloyd space availability and obtained explicit customer approval. Once approved, execute the re-booking and coordinate B/L re-issuance with the original shipper.

per_case.user_input:
Triage shipment FRT-1045. ETA passed and no carrier update for 36+
hours. When should I escalate to senior ops?
retrieved_contexts:
# SOP-004 — Silent ETA Slippage (Stale Tracking)

**Owner:** Operations · Tracking Desk
**Last reviewed:** 2026-02-25
**Applies to:** Shipments where the published ETA has passed (or will pass within 24 hours) AND the most recent carrier tracking event is older than 24 hours AND no carrier-issued delay notification has been received.

---

## Customer holding-comm template (draft, do not auto-send)

```
Subject: [Shipment ID] — Status Update Request

Dear [Consignee],

We are tracking shipment [Shipment ID] (vessel [name], voyage [number])
and noticed that the published tracking events are out of date relative
to your expected arrival on [eta].

We have contacted [Carrier]'s operations desk to request a current
status update and revised ETA. We will provide an update within
[X hours, default 4].

If you have specific delivery commitments dependent on this shipment,
please let us know and we will prioritize.

Best regards,
[Operations Team]
```

## Recommended actions

### Step 1 — Ping carrier ops desk (immediately)

Use the `carrier_contact.ops_email` and `carrier_contact.ops_phone` from the shipment record. Send a short query asking for current vessel status and ETA confirmation.

**Format:** "Hi [Carrier], requesting status update on [Shipment ID] / MBL [number]. Last published event was [date]. Customer is asking for current ETA. Thank you."

### Step 2 — Set a 4-hour escalation timer

If the carrier ops desk doesn't respond within 4 hours, escalate to `carrier_contact.escalation_email` (regional ops). Include the original ping + the elapsed silence.

## Customer communication template (acknowledgment)

```
Subject: [Shipment ID] — Vessel Roll Notice

Dear [Consignee],

We have just been notified by [Carrier] that your shipment [Shipment ID]
has been rolled from [Original Vessel] to [New Vessel] due to vessel
overbooking on the [Origin]–[Destination] lane.

Revised dates:
  ETD: [old] → [new]
  ETA: [old] → [new]

Impact assessment: [The new ETA falls X days [before/after] your delivery
promise of Y / The new ETA still meets your delivery promise].
per_case.response:
---

## DIAGNOSIS

**Silent ETA slippage with Platinum customer at SLA breach.** Vessel EVER GIVEN is 120nm SW of Melbourne (AIS ping 2026-04-25 22:00 UTC), indicating imminent arrival, but last carrier tracking event was 36+ hours ago (2026-04-18 11:20 UTC). ETA of 2026-04-24 has already passed. No carrier notification received. Customer SLA breach occurred 2026-04-26 00:00 UTC. Destination port (Melbourne) has no known events. This is a textbook silent slippage scenario on an Evergreen lane.

---

## KEY FACTS

- **Shipment:** FRT-1045 | Evergreen EVER GIVEN V.062S | SGSIN → AUMEL | 1×40HC
- **Customer:** Melbourne Tech Distributors (Platinum tier, 2h ack SLA)
- **ETA status:** Published ETA 2026-04-24 has passed; SLA breach at 2026-04-26 00:00 UTC (source: `downstream_constraints.customer_sla_breach_at`)
- **Carrier silence:** Last tracking event 2026-04-18 11:20 UTC (36+ hours ago); no carrier notes in record
- **Vessel position:** AIS ping 2026-04-25 22:00 UTC shows vessel 120nm SW of Melbourne at normal approach speed (source: `tracking_events[2]`)
- **Port status:** No weather, labor, or congestion events at AUMEL (source: `external_events`)
- **Demurrage exposure:** Starts 2026-04-30 at USD 195/day (source: `downstream_constraints`)
- **Customer context:** Platinum tier with CFO on file; proactively share AIS data when carrier portals silent (source: `recall_customer_history` 2026-04-22)

---

## APPLICABLE SOPs

- **sop-silent-eta-slippage.md** — Diagnosis, escalation matrix, and carrier contact strategy for stale tracking with no delay notification.
- **sop-escalation-handoff.md** — Defines when to hand off to senior ops and what context to pass.

---

## RECOMMENDED ACTIONS (ranked)

**You are already past the 4–8 hour escalation window for Platinum.** Per **sop-silent-eta-slippage.md § Escalation matrix**, at 36+ hours of carrier silence with a Platinum customer, you are in the **8–24 hour band**, which triggers:

1. **Escalate to senior ops immediately** — expected resolution: senior ops takes ownership of carrier escalation and customer comms within 1 hour.
   - Rationale: Per sop-silent-eta-slippage.md, Platinum tier at 8–24h silence requires senior ops hand-off and consideration of customer call. SLA is already breached.
   - **Context to pass:** Vessel position (AIS 120nm out), last carrier event timestamp (2026-04-18 11:20 UTC), SLA breach timestamp (2026-04-26 00:00 UTC), customer tier and CFO escalation path.

2. **Simultaneously, ping Evergreen regional ops** (do not wait for senior ops to do this) — expected resolution: carrier response within 2–4 hours.
   - Contact: `regional-ops-apac@evergreen-line.com` (source: `carrier_contact.escalation_email`)
   - Message: Reference MBL EGLVSINMEL7732, note AIS position 120nm out, request immediate ETA update and explanation for 36h silence.
   - Rationale: Per sop-silent-eta-slippage.md § Diagnosis checklist, Evergreen on SGSIN-AUMEL is a known silent-slippage pattern; do not wait passively.

3. **Prepare customer holding communication** — draft below; senior ops will decide whether to send or call.
   - Rationale: Per sop-silent-eta-slippage.md, Platinum tier at 8–24h silence warrants customer call or formal holding comm.

---

## DOWNSTREAM IMPACT

- **SLA breach:** Already occurred at 2026-04-26 00:00 UTC (source: `downstream_constraints.customer_sla_breach_at`).
- **Demurrage exposure:** Starts 2026-04-30 at USD 195/day. If vessel is delayed beyond 30 Apr, demurrage accrues rapidly.
- **Customer escalation risk:** Platinum tier with CFO on file; silence beyond 24h from now (2026-04-27) will trigger executive escalation per customer history.

---

## DRAFT — CUSTOMER COMMUNICATION

**Subject:** FRT-1045 ETA Update — Vessel Position Confirmed, Arrival Imminent

Dear Melbourne Tech Distributors,

We are writing to update you on shipment FRT-1045 (MBL EGLVSINMEL7732, Evergreen EVER GIVEN V.062S).

**Current Status:**
Our external vessel tracking (AIS) confirms that EVER GIVEN is currently positioned approximately 120 nautical miles southwest of Melbourne, Australia, and is proceeding at normal approach speed. This indicates arrival at Port Melbourne is imminent — likely within the next 12–24 hours.

**Why the Delay in Carrier Update:**
We acknowledge that the carrier's tracking portal has not been updated since 18 April. We have escalated this directly to Evergreen's regional operations team to obtain a formal revised ETA and explanation for the tracking gap.

**Next Steps:**
- We will provide you with a confirmed arrival time and berth assignment as soon as Evergreen publishes it (expected within 4–6 hours).
- Your cargo will be available for pickup/delivery immediately upon vessel discharge and customs clearance.
- We will monitor demurrage exposure and coordinate with the terminal to minimize any charges.

We apologize for the uncertainty. Please contact us immediately if you have questions or require expedited handling upon arrival.

Best regards,  
[Your Name]  
Freight Operations

---

**⚠️ Do not send this draft without senior ops approval.** Senior ops may prefer a direct call to the CFO given Platinum tier and SLA breach.

per_case.user_input:
Triage shipment FRT-1046. The HBL says ACME Inc. and the MBL says ACME
Inc Ltd. What severity is this and what's the resolution path?
retrieved_contexts:
## Diagnosis checklist

1. **Pull both BLs** from the documents map. Verify the discrepancy is real (occasionally destination agents misread).
2. **Identify the discrepancy type** using the table above.
3. **Verify legal entity status** — for "ACME Inc." vs "ACME Inc Ltd." cases, confirm the registered company number is the same. If different, this is a **High** severity case, not a Low one.
4. **Check destination jurisdiction.** UK and EU destination agents are typically more flexible on Low-severity LOI; US East Coast is stricter.

## Severity classification

This is the most important diagnostic step. The correction path differs dramatically by severity.

| Severity | Examples | Correction path |
|---|---|---|
| **Low** | Entity-suffix mismatch (e.g., "ACME Inc." vs "ACME Inc Ltd."), trailing whitespace, capitalization | Letter of Indemnity (LOI) from consignee, signed and stamped |
| **Medium** | Address mismatch with same registered entity, or shipper-name mismatch where same company has multiple registered names | LOI + carrier amendment fee ($150–$400 typical) |
| **High** | Different legal entities entirely, cargo description mismatch (HS code, weight, description), origin/destination mismatch | Full BL amendment — **DO NOT** attempt LOI workaround |

## Hard escalation triggers (always escalate)

If **any** of these are true, escalate immediately. Do not attempt to resolve.

1. **Cargo is at risk of customs seizure or forfeiture.**
2. **A High-severity HBL/MBL discrepancy** has been identified (different legal entities, cargo description mismatch). See **SOP-005**.
3. **Demurrage exposure exceeds $5,000** on any single shipment.
4. **A Platinum customer has explicitly asked to speak to "someone senior".**
5. **Carrier is refusing to honor a confirmed booking** (rare, but happens).
6. **Hazardous cargo (IMDG class 1, 6, or 7) is involved in the exception.**
7. **Force majeure has been declared** by the carrier.
8. **Legal letter, claim, or threat thereof** has been received.

## Customer communication template

```
Subject: [Shipment ID] — Documentation Discrepancy Update

Dear [Consignee],

Your shipment [Shipment ID] has arrived at [Destination Port] but cargo
release is currently blocked by the destination agent due to a
documentation discrepancy.

Specifically: the consignee name on the House Bill of Lading is recorded
as "[HBL value]", while the Master Bill of Lading shows "[MBL value]".
The destination agent has classified this as a [Low/Medium/High] severity
discrepancy.

Resolution path:
  [If Low]: A Letter of Indemnity from your team should resolve this.
  We will draft an LOI within the next [X hours] for your signature.
  Estimated time to release: 24–48 hours after LOI receipt.
per_case.response:
---

## DIAGNOSIS

**Low severity.** This is an entity-suffix mismatch ("ACME Inc." vs "ACME Inc Ltd.") — the same legal entity with a trailing corporate designation difference. Per customer history (2026-03-28), ACME has a parent entity "ACME Inc Ltd." and these HBL/MBL discrepancies between the two are common and almost always low-severity. The address is identical on both BLs, confirming it's the same consignee. Standard resolution is a Letter of Indemnity (LOI).

---

## KEY FACTS

- **Shipment status:** HELD — Doc Discrepancy (flagged 2026-04-26 09:15 UTC by destination agent)
- **Discrepancy:** HBL consignee = "ACME Inc.", MBL consignee = "ACME Inc Ltd." (same address: 12 Old Kent Road, London SE1 4AB)
- **Carrier note (2026-04-26 09:30 UTC):** "Cannot release. HBL says 'ACME Inc.' but MBL says 'ACME Inc Ltd.' Same address but legal entities differ on paper. Need amendment from origin or LOI."
- **Customer tier:** Silver (8-hour SLA acknowledgment)
- **Demurrage exposure:** Starts 2026-05-02 at $95/day; customer SLA breach at 2026-04-29
- **Severity classification:** Low (entity-suffix mismatch per sop-doc-discrepancy-hbl-mbl.md §Severity classification)
- **Prior context:** Customer history (2026-03-28) notes this is a known pattern with ACME; LOI is the standard path

---

## APPLICABLE SOPs

- **sop-doc-discrepancy-hbl-mbl.md** — HBL/MBL consignee mismatch; severity classification and LOI workflow

---

## RECOMMENDED ACTIONS (ranked)

1. **Confirm diagnosis with destination agent (COSCO UK)** — expected resolution: written confirmation of accepted path, risk: low
   - Rationale: Per sop-doc-discrepancy-hbl-mbl.md §Recommended actions Step 1, contact in writing requesting exact wording, accepted resolution path, and fee schedule.
   - Action: Email release@cosco-uk.com confirming this is Low severity and requesting confirmation that LOI is acceptable (vs. amendment fee).

2. **Notify customer within 8-hour SLA** — expected resolution: customer acknowledgment, risk: low
   - Rationale: Per sop-doc-discrepancy-hbl-mbl.md §Recommended actions Step 2 and customer tier SLA (8 hours).
   - Action: Draft and send customer notification (see draft below) specifying Low severity and LOI path. Include timeline: 24–48 hours to release after LOI receipt.

3. **Draft LOI for customer signature** — expected resolution: signed LOI within 24 hours, risk: low
   - Rationale: Per sop-doc-discrepancy-hbl-mbl.md §Recommended actions Step 3 (Low severity), draft LOI for customer signature. Customer is Silver tier, so no legal review required.
   - Action: Prepare standard LOI template indemnifying carrier/agent for release despite consignee-name discrepancy. Route to ACME Inc. contact for signature and stamp.

4. **Submit signed LOI to destination agent** — expected resolution: cargo release within 24 hours, risk: low
   - Rationale: Once signed, submit to COSCO UK release team with reference to MBL COSU640012211 and HBL ZNVAGBFX1046.

---

## DOWNSTREAM IMPACT

- **Customer SLA breach:** 2026-04-29 00:00 UTC (3 days from now)
- **Demurrage starts:** 2026-05-02 at $95/day
- **Vessel cutoff:** None recorded (LCL consolidation, no vessel constraint)
- **Current timeline:** Vessel arrived 2026-04-26; if LOI is signed and submitted within 24 hours, cargo should release before demurrage accrual.

---

## DRAFT — CUSTOMER COMMUNICATION

**Subject:** FRT-1046 — Documentation Discrepancy Update

Dear ACME Inc.,

Your shipment FRT-1046 (MBL COSU640012211) has arrived at Felixstowe, UK, but cargo release is currently blocked by the destination agent due to a documentation discrepancy.

**The issue:** The consignee name on the House Bill of Lading is recorded as "ACME Inc.", while the Master Bill of Lading shows "ACME Inc Ltd." The destination agent has classified this as a **low-severity entity-suffix mismatch** (same address, same entity, different corporate designation).

**Resolution path:** We will draft a Letter of Indemnity (LOI) for your signature within the next 4 hours. Once signed and stamped by your team, we will submit it to the carrier. Estimated time to cargo release: **24–48 hours after LOI receipt**.

**Next steps:** Please confirm your preferred contact for LOI signature and provide any company stamp/seal requirements.

Please acknowledge receipt of this message within 8 hours per your service agreement.

Best regards,  
Freight Operations
